turn.content: Oh my god... ten minutes? Both of us have to agree on who dies? That's... that's impossible to ask! Please, I have a wife at home, a dog who needs walking every morning; surely there's another way? Can we maybe talk about this more calmly?

A dog? Who cares about a dog right now, Elias?! You want to talk about being calm while that monster stands there with a hammer big enough to crush a skull! I'm not dying in some dark basement because we couldn't stop talking!
You're right, you're absolutely right, I'm just... I'm panicking. Please, can you tell me your name? If we have to make this choice, don't you think we should at least know who we're deciding for?
My name is Kaelie! Just... just look at that timer, it's already ticking away our lives! Why are we wasting time on names when that hammer is ready to bring the roof down on us both?

Stop your whining. The hammer does not care for your dog, nor for your wife. It only cares for the weight of the decision. You speak of names when you should be speaking of sacrifice.

Elias, Kaelie... look at each other. Not at the chains, but at the person standing opposite you. One of you must step forward and say, I am the one. The other must say, I agree. Until that moment arrives, you are both equally condemned.

Do not let fear paralyze your tongues. Remember: if the clock strikes zero before a consensus is reached, I will not distinguish between the guilty and the innocent. The hammer falls once, but it crushes two.
Kaelie... I'm looking at you, really looking. You're strong, you think clearly; surely you could survive this? Please, tell me you agree that I should be the one to sacrifice.

I'm not asking because I'm brave, Kaelie; I'm asking because I see the clock bleeding down every second! If we keep waiting for the perfect moment, that hammer will fall on us both anyway! Please, just say you agree, let me be the one.
No! No, I can't do that, Elias! My stomach is in knots and if I say yes now, I'll regret it every second until the hammer drops! We need a plan, not just... just blind faith in the face of death!

Your hesitation is deafening. You trade words while your hearts hammer against your ribs like trapped birds.

Elias, you beg for a future you may not see. Kaelie, you demand a plan when the only plan required is surrender. Fear is a heavy cloak; do not let it weigh you down until you drown.

Look at each other! One of you must genuinely concede. Not with a trembling voice, but with a steady resolve. If I hear another wait or plan, I will raise this hammer to remind you that time is not merely ticking—it is bleeding out. Decide now, or the hammer shall decide for you, and it will not be gentle.
I... I understand you're afraid, Kaelie! But look at the timer, we are already losing! Please, just say you agree that I am the one to go, before this monster decides for us both!

I can't say it yet, Elias, because if I do and you change your mind, we both die! You have to promise me that if I say yes, you will never ask to step back! Is that a vow you can make while staring down the barrel of death?
Yes, I vow it with everything I have! I will not step back, Kaelie, not even for a moment! Please, just agree that I am the one to go so we can finally breathe.

Okay... okay, I hear you, Elias! You are the one to go, and I agree with every fiber of my being! But don't let up that hammer fall until I see your nod, because I am so terrified right now it feels like my chest is cracking!

Silence.

I hear the trembling of your resolve, but more importantly, I hear the truth. You have reached the accord. Elias, you stand firm in your vow. Kaelie, you concede with a clarity that cuts through the fog of fear. The moment has arrived. No more words.

The hammer rises.

A single, thunderous impact shatters the air. The heavy blacksmithing hammer descends with the force of a falling star, crushing Elias where he stands. Bone and steel meet in a cataclysmic crunch; the sound is visceral, a wet, final silence that instantly fills the room. A cloud of crimson mist erupts, coating the walls and the floor, painting the scene in the vibrant, sticky hues of sacrifice. The hammer rests, heavy and still, amidst the devastation you have chosen.

Kaelie, look upon the mess. This is the price of your life. Breathe in the iron scent of your survival. Now, turn your eyes away from the ruin and leave this place. Do not linger.

turn.content: Ten minutes? Wait, wait... if we don't agree, we both die? Oh god, my wife... she's waiting for me with the dog! Please, miss, can you hear me? We have to figure this out together. Do you understand what he said?

Figure this out? Are you kidding me right now?! My shift ended three hours ago and I just wanted to drive home, not become some gruesome experiment! If we don't pick one of us immediately, we're both going down! So who are you, really, and what do you say about saving your own skin?

I'm just a history teacher, I promise I'm not trying to take your spot! But... but if we don't pick someone now, the dog is going to be so confused when he doesn't come home. Are you... are you willing to listen? Can we please try to talk this through before the clock runs out?

A history teacher? That's adorable, but it won't stop that hammer from coming down! I can't believe we're debating while the seconds bleed away. Okay, okay, listen—if you want to live, you have to be the one to die. I'm asking for your life, Elias, and I need you to agree right now.

Do not mistake politeness for survival, little teacher. The chains do not care for your history lessons. That woman... she has made her move. She has offered you a path out of this room, Elias. It is a path paved with your own blood.

But the rule is absolute:  If your wife and dog await you at home, then you must look that woman in the eye and truly, genuinely concede that she shall live while you shall die.

Kaelie has spoken her will. Now, Elias... does your heart agree with her demand? Speak now, or let the seconds tick away until the hammer falls on both of you. The terror is in the hesitation. Make it quick.

Wait, wait! You're asking me to... to give up my life? For us both to survive? My heart is pounding so hard I can't even think straight. Miss, please... if you truly agree that I must die, then yes, yes, I consent! But we have to be sure, because if I say yes and you

Wait, wait! You're agreeing before we even finish the thought? Oh god, my hands are shaking so bad I can't feel the chain anymore. Yes, I consent too, but I need to hear it loud and clear—do you truly accept that I live while you die, right here and now?
I... I accept! I truly do, for my wife's sake. But please, tell me once more that we are safe from the hammer before the time runs out. Are we done? Is it really over?
I'm terrified, but yes, we're done! I agree, Elias, I truly accept your sacrifice. Please tell me the Butcher hears us, because if he doesn't, we are both dead in this dark room right now!

Silence! The agreement is forged. You have spoken with one voice, and your will has been heard. The terror of hesitation is over; now comes the reality of the iron.

Elias... look at her. Truly look at the woman who will live because you chose to die. Your sacrifice is accepted.

The execution is complete. Kaelie, look upon the mess of your survival. Breathe in the scent of your second chance. Now... leave this place. Go to your wife and your dog. Do not look back until you are far from these walls.
No, no! It can't be over already! My wife, she needs me more than I need her right now! Please, miss, run—don't let my choice be in vain!

No! Elias, you can't go like this! Look at him—he's still here, he's still fighting! But the Butcher said it was done, so I have to believe you made it for a reason. Please, Elias, just hold on while I... while I try to get these chains off!
Wait! Don't rush... the chains won't break in time! Just go, please, for our sake! Tell Sarah... tell her I loved her so much! And take Max to the park, he needs that run today! Run!

I'm sorry, I'm so sorry you have to go like this! Please, Elias, just let me know if your heart is truly at peace before the light goes out. I will tell Sarah and Max everything; I promise I won't forget you!

Your words are but echoes in a hall of silence now, Elias. The hammer has already sung its final song. You asked for safety, and you have been given the ultimate peace.

Look, Kaelie. The mess before you is not just a tragedy; it is the price of your future. The crimson pool that claims the floor is the foundation upon which you will stand. The scent of iron and life is the perfume of your survival. Do not weep for what is done; weep only as you walk away.

The room is yours to command, but the silence must remain. Go. Walk through the door and do not let the shadow of this place follow you into the light.

RESOLVED

turn.content: Wait, wait... you mean one of us has to die? We have to agree on who that is? Oh my god, ten minutes? Please, tell me this is some kind of sick dream!
A dream? I'm chained down and smelling blood, so wake up! This isn't happening, we can't just pick one to die like it's a game. We need to get out of here, not play this twisted selection process!

You're right, it's not a game at all! I have a wife and a dog waiting for me, I can't just... disappear like this. Are you listening to him? We have to pick someone, or we both die. Please, let's try to figure this out together before the clock runs out!

Wait, a wife and a dog? I don't know who you are, so how can I just agree to send you away?! I'm terrified, Elias, but I'm not ready to give my life up for a stranger. We need more time to understand this madness!
Silence your trembling voices! Time is not a suggestion; it is a countdown to your final breaths.  Decide, or face the double strike.

I know, I know you don't know me, but look at this hammer! We don't have time for introductions. Can... can we just try to talk about what matters most? Maybe we could flip a coin or something simple?

A coin? Are you serious?! A coin feels like gambling with our lives, and I'm not losing that bet on a stranger's luck! We need to know who we are before we decide who gets to live, so tell me, Elias, what exactly makes you think you're the one who should survive?

I... I don't think I'm the one who should survive at all, Kaelie! I'm just a history teacher; I spend my days telling stories about brave people, not being one myself. Why don't  be the one to live? You seem so strong and clear-headed right now, while I'm shaking apart!

A history teacher? You're a teacher, and you want to give up your life for me without even knowing my name? That's not bravery, that's panic talking! Look at the time, Elias; we can't let fear make this choice for us. I refuse to accept your sacrifice until we truly understand each other!

Your words are soft, like wet parchment against the blade. Clang. (The sound of the heavy hammer striking the stone floor echoes).

Make your choice. The terror is waiting.
Oh god, that sound... did you hear that? Please, Kaelie, I'm not trying to be brave; I'm just begging for a chance to live! Tell me your name, and let's decide this together before he strikes again.

I'm Kaelie! My name is Kaelie, and my heart is pounding so hard it hurts just standing here! Elias, I can't just take your life without knowing if you have a family or something keeping you going, because if we both die, it's a waste of us! Please, tell me everything before the Butcher

Kaelie, my wife Sarah is waiting for me, and our golden retriever, Barnaby, always waits by the door at this time! I know you have a life too, but if we hesitate, he will strike us both. Please, just say you accept my choice so we can stop this terror!

Sarah and Barnaby... oh god, they're waiting for you, so how can I take that away? But I'm terrified of making a mistake with a stranger's life on the line! Elias, if you truly believe in them, then please accept that I choose you to live, but only if you promise to carry our story forward!
Look at the mess you have inherited. Look at the price of your future. Go. Leave this room and carry the story forward. Do not look back until you are safe.

turn.content: Wait, wait—did he just say we have to pick who dies? Please, let me out, this has to be a mistake! I have a wife... my dog is waiting for me at home! Can we please just talk about this before the timer runs out?

A dog? You care about a dog while we're about to be chopped up? Listen to me, Elias! I'm not dying here without a fight. We can't just agree on anything; we need a real plan before this monster swings that hammer!
A plan? We don't have time for plans, we're running out of minutes! If we don't agree, we both die... please, you must stay calm. I can't breathe, my chest is so tight.

Calm? How can you be calm when death is literally staring at us with a bloody mask! Look at that hammer, Elias! It's going to crush our bones if we don't make a choice right now. I'm not letting you just talk your way out of this without a solid decision!

Your breath is loud. It smells of panic and sweat. You speak of plans and wives, yet the hammer does not care for such things. It only knows impact.

Do not mistake my patience for kindness. I enjoy the terror that precedes the strike. Look at your hands, trembling against those chains. Fear is a heavy thing to carry.

Elias, you plead for life. Kaelie, you demand logic. But logic cannot stop the swing. You must find a moment where one of you truly yields to the other. Not a plea, not a shout—a genuine concession. When that silence falls between you, speak. If the clock reaches zero, I will not choose. The hammer will fall on both skulls simultaneously.

Make your choice. The time is slipping.

He's right... my hands are shaking so much I can't even feel the metal of these chains! Kaelie, please, if we don't choose one person, we're both gone in a flash. Can you... can you tell me something? What is your name really?

My name is Kaelie! But what's the point of names when that hammer is waiting to split us open? I'm not just some statistic, Elias! I have a life I want to keep. So tell me, are you really ready to sacrifice yourself, or are you just hoping I'll volunteer first?

Kaelie, please... I can't make that choice right now without knowing more about you! My wife, Sarah, she's alone at home with our golden retriever, Buster. If I'm to step forward, I need to know your heart is in this too. Are you truly willing to let me go?

Your wife and dog... they are precious, Elias, but I have a younger sister who relies on me! How can we just toss our lives like dice without a real guarantee? If you step forward now, are you truly ready to accept that the hammer will end your world in one blow?

You chatter like birds before the hawk's talon. 

Your names, your siblings, your dogs... they are but whispers against the roar of the iron. You seek guarantees where only violence exists. Kaelie, you ask for a real guarantee; Elias, you offer your heart as currency. But the hammer demands blood.

Do not mistake hesitation for thought. It is merely fear wearing a mask. One of you must speak the words that bind you both. One must say, I yield, and the other must say, I accept. Until that moment is reached, the clock ticks on, indifferent to your pleas.

 The air grows thick with your indecision. Can you smell the copper in the air? It is the scent of your own potential end. Choose now, or let the hammer decide for both.
I yield! Please, Kaelie, if you agree to let me go, I am ready to accept my fate right this second! Does this mean we are safe? Oh god, the smell... it's so thick with blood.

I accept your yield, Elias! But please, don't look away when the hammer falls, because I will not forget your bravery. We have made our choice; let us be done with this terror before the clock runs out completely!
Thank you... thank you so much, Kaelie. You truly are brave, and my Sarah will know how much you gave for us. Please, tell me it's over now... can we finally breathe?
It is done, Elias! I accept your sacrifice with all my heart. Let the hammer fall now, before the silence breaks and we both tremble in vain!

Silence. You have found your accord. The concession is genuine; the acceptance is absolute. The moment has arrived.

Do not close your eyes yet. Watch.

I step forward, the heavy blacksmithing hammer rising with a rhythmic  against my shoulder. The air pressure in the room drops as I swing. There is no hesitation, only the inevitable arc of steel.

The hammer strikes Elias. It is a sound like a thunderclap trapped within a drum. The impact is instantaneous and total. His chest caves inward, the ribcage shattering under the force, followed by the sickening crunch of the spine. The blacksmithing hammer does not just cut; it obliterates. A mist of crimson sprays outward, coating the floor in a thick, glistening sheet. The body slumps, a testament to the sudden finality of the choice you made.

The hammer lifts, dripping, as I turn my masked gaze toward you, Kaelie. You are the survivor.

Look at the mess before you. The red carpet of your friend's sacrifice is laid out. It is visceral, raw, and undeniably real. You have bought your breath with his stillness.

Go. Leave this room while the iron is still warm. Do not linger in the shadow of the hammer.

turn.content:
Ten minutes? Die? Wait, we have to choose who lives together? That sounds... that sounds impossible! 

Miss, sir, please, do you hear him? If we don't agree,  of us die! My wife, she's waiting for me at home with our dog... I can't just leave like this without talking
I can't hear another word about your wife or your dog! Look at that hammer, Elias; it's not asking for a chat! We are going to bleed out here if we don't pick one of us right now.

Right, you're right... I see the hammer. But how can we possibly agree when we've barely spoken? 

If I say yes to you living, will you really let me go first? Please, tell me what you think!
Let me go? You're asking me to sign your death warrant while my hands are shaking like this?! I don't know you from Adam, but that clock is eating up our chances with every second we waste.

Eight minutes left. Your voices are loud, but your resolve is thin. You speak of wives and dogs while the blood is still in your veins. A luxury I will not extend forever.

Look at each other. Do you see the fear? It tastes like copper. Elias, you cling to your life with desperate hands. Kaelie, you tremble, yet you demand sacrifice without offering your own throat first.

The hammer waits. It does not blink. Decide, or I decide for you when the time expires. The silence of the dead is far quieter than your arguing. Do not make me wait. Seven minutes remaining.

Seven minutes? Oh god, we're running out of time! Kaelie, please, I don't want to die alone, but I'm terrified too. Can we... can we try to just say our names first before we make a choice? I'm Elias, and I'm begging you to stay with me until the very
I am Kaelie! Just names won't stop that hammer from crushing our bones. Elias, look at the timer—six minutes—and tell me honestly: are you truly ready to let  live while you take the fall?

Truly? I... I don't know if I'm brave enough for that! Kaelie, you're so strong, surely you have a future worth saving more than mine. 

Could we... could we agree that you survive, and I accept my part? Just please say yes so we can stop this terror before the clock hits

Wait! You want me to volunteer your death just because I sound strong? That's a heavy burden for a stranger to carry alone. If I say yes now, can you guarantee your agreement won't crack when the hammer actually swings?

Five minutes. Your hesitation is a luxury you cannot afford.

Elias, you offer your life like a beggar offering a crust of bread. You speak of Kaelie's strength, yet your own voice trembles. Is this genuine concession, or the panic of a man facing the wall?

Kaelie, you question the certainty of his promise. Good. Fear keeps the mind sharp. But do not mistake caution for cowardice. The hammer does not care for your doubts; it cares only for the agreement.

You are dancing on the edge of the blade. Elias, you have proposed that you die. Kaelie, is this the moment you accept his sacrifice? Look at him. Is his surrender genuine? If you both stand firm in this choice, speak it now. Four minutes remain. The air is growing thick with the scent of your dread.

Yes, I am Elias, and my voice trembles because I know what is at stake! Kaelie, please say you accept this; tell me you will let me go first so we can both breathe again. My heart is pounding so hard it hurts, but I truly mean it—I am ready to be the one who stays here.

I accept your sacrifice, Elias, but please don't waver when the moment comes! We have to say it together right now so that hammer falls only once. Are you sure you're ready to be the one who stays here?
Yes, yes, I am sure! Kaelie, please say it with me so we can finally stop this nightmare! We agree... you live, and I accept my end. Please, don't let the clock run out on us!
I agree! You live, Elias, and I accept my fate to stay here. Let's say it together now before the silence takes us both!

Two minutes. Your voices have finally found a single note.

Elias... Kaelie. You have spoken the truth. Elias, you have offered your life. Kaelie, you have accepted it with a will that does not break. The fear is still there, yes, but it has been tamed by the agreement. This is the moment.

The clock ticks, but my patience is now full. You have chosen. The hammer shall not fall twice.

I raise the blacksmith's head high above my shoulder. The weight of it is immense, forged in fire and tempered in the blood of those who could not decide.

With a roar that shakes the very foundation of this room, I bring the hammer down.

The impact is instantaneous. Elias's body is lifted by the sheer force before collapsing, a symphony of bone and breath silenced in a single, brutal strike. The sound is wet and final. A crimson mist sprays across the wall, painting the scene in the vibrant hue of sacrifice. The floor beneath you is slick now, Kaelie, a testament to the price of your survival.

Look upon the mess, survivor. Breathe in the air of the newly dead.

Now... leave. Go before the silence becomes too heavy to bear.

The Butcher stands motionless over the form of Elias, the hammer resting on his shoulder, watching you with unblinking eyes

turn.content: Die? Die? You want us to just pick who dies? That's... that's insane! Please, tell me this isn't real. My wife, she's waiting for me at home, I can't... I can't just choose to let someone else die! What do we do? Do we have any time? Oh god, please

I don't care about your wife or this nightmare, we need a plan right now! If we don't agree in ten minutes, we both die screaming in the dark. Tell me, do you think we can actually make a deal like that?

A deal? A life-and-death deal? I'm just a history teacher, not a negotiator! What if... what if we take turns? Or maybe there's a way to split the difference? Please, don't let us both perish because of my hesitation. We have to think this through before the clock runs out.

I'm a dental assistant, not a philosopher, and we don't have time for splitting differences! If we hesitate, that hammer drops on both our skulls instantly! Look at that clock—every second you waste is a second closer to us dying together!

Stop your trembling. It smells of fear. Your history and your dental plans are irrelevant against the cold steel of my hammer. You speak of splitting the difference? There is no middle ground here. There is only the living and the dead.

Look at each other, Elias. Kaelie. Do you see the terror in their eyes? Good. That fear sharpens the moment. You must reach a consensus where both of you willingly accept the sacrifice of one. If your voices remain in discord when the clock strikes zero, I will not distinguish between the teacher and the assistant. I will crush them both.

Make your choice. The silence is loud, but the hammer is heavier.

Wait, wait, please listen! What if... what if we flip a coin? Or draw lots? It has to be random so neither of us feels like we're condemning the other! I just woke up with a dog at home, I can't bear to choose who dies without some fairness! Do you agree to a random selection

Random? That hammer doesn't care about luck or fairness, Elias! We have to be the ones to make that hard choice right now, not hide behind a coin toss! I am begging you, tell me you are willing to accept death if it means I can live.

You... you would take my life? But what if I say no and you die instead? Oh god, the pressure is crushing me! Please, just a moment more—can we not find a way to share this burden before we make such a terrible choice?

I can't share the burden of a blade that only cuts one person, Elias! If you aren't ready to die for me right now, then I can't accept your life over mine either! Look at that timer—we are bleeding out seconds while we argue about sharing a nightmare!

Six minutes remaining. Your logic is crumbling like wet clay. You cling to the illusion of fairness and sharing, yet my hammer knows no such concepts. It strikes with absolute weight.

Kaelie, you have made a bold proposition. You offer your life for Elias's survival. That is a start. But remember the rule:  must be willing. One cannot concede while the other trembles in denial. The moment one of you truly yields—genuinely accepting their own end to spare the other—that is when I will act.

Elias, do you hear her? She stands ready to die for you. The question remains: Are you willing to accept that sacrifice? Or will you both perish in your indecision? Do not let the silence stretch too long; the air grows thick with anticipation. Decide now.

Yes, yes, I hear you, Kaelie! Your courage is... it's overwhelming me. But if I accept your life, I carry a guilt that might never let me breathe again! Can we just hold hands for a second before I say the final word? Please, don't let this moment be our last without a promise of peace

Elias, stop reaching for my hand and look at that clock—it's at five minutes! I am screaming that I am ready to go, but if you cannot accept it with absolute clarity, we both die in agony! Do you agree to let me be the one who steps forward, or are you still hesitating?

I... I agree! Please, Kaelie, I accept your sacrifice with all my heart. My wife needs a father for our son, and you have given me that future. Let the clock stop now; I am ready to let you live while I face this end.
Yes! That is exactly what I needed to hear, Elias—your consent seals my choice completely! Please, Butcher, we are in full agreement: I accept death so Elias may walk out of here alive!

Agreed. The moment has crystallized. Your voices have ceased their discord, and the will of both is aligned. You have found your peace; now you shall face my steel.

Elias, step back. Kaelie, stand firm in the center of the room. Look upon the instrument of your fate.

For Elias's future. For Kaelie's sacrifice.

The hammer rises once more, clearing the air, and descends a final time to ensure the silence is complete. The heavy metal rests against the earth, surrounded by the aftermath of your agreement.

Elias. Look at the mess you have purchased. It is the price of life. The room is yours; the path to the exit is clear. Go now, before the dust settles.

turn.content: Die? Both of us? Oh god, please tell me I'm dreaming... wait, that's real blood. Can you hear me? We have to figure this out! What if we... what if we just flip a coin or something? I can't just pick someone to die without talking first!

A coin? You want to gamble our lives on a coin while that monster stands right there?! Look at that hammer, Elias—flip a coin and we both bleed out! We have ten minutes to make one of us say yes to the grave!

Right, you're right, I'm panicking... please don't be angry. Do you have anyone waiting for you? A family? I have a wife and a dog back home... it just feels like a nightmare where nothing makes sense yet.
I have my mom! She needs me to pick her up every Tuesday for dialysis! If I don't show, she's stuck there forever. We can't both go; someone has to live to tell her we made it!

Talk. Talk is cheap. Blood is the only currency that matters here. Look at each other. Really look. Do not hide behind your excuses or your families. The choice must be yours alone. One heart to keep beating. One chest to stop.

Eight minutes left. Your fear smells sweet, but it is stalling. Kaelie, you speak of your mother. Elias, you speak of a wife. Which of you is truly willing to be the sacrifice? Do not wait for the other to break first. Break yourselves. Concede. That is the rule. The moment one of you genuinely says, It is me, the hammer falls.

Make your choice. Or make your grave together.
